channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-23
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. Notably, the `statcheck` and `escicheck` projects have both received updates, with `statcheck` incorporating admin updates and `escicheck` synchronizing with a new version of `escicheckapp`, highlighting the connections between these research tools. Meanwhile, `docpluck` has addressed several issues, including rendering and documentation updates, while `oddpub` has improved its ontogenerator's code validation. Additionally, `coarse` and `regcheck` have both made fixes to their web applications, with `coarse` re-enabling Fable 5 in the model picker and `regcheck` resolving issues with its demo page, card preview, and survey routes. These updates demonstrate the community's commitment to improving and maintaining these valuable research tools.

21 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

New releases: oddpub v7.3.0

Most active:

tenzing7 commits
oddpub7 commits
metacheck6 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `data_check` branch was the focus of recent work, with the addition of a "reproducibility_check" as stated in the commit message "add reproducibility_check". This change, made on July 22, 2026, specifically added this new check to the repository. On July 18, 2026, another commit to the `data_check` branch added "japs jamovi", as per the commit message "add japs jamovi", which suggests the integration of JASP and Jamovi functionality. The `data_check` branch was the sole branch worked on during this period. No other branches were modified in these commits.

1 commit(s) today:

  • db4cf0f [data_check] add reproducibility_check — Lakens
  • 0612101 [data_check] add japs jamovi — Lakens
  • 1c49ec7 [dev] Merge pull request #333 from scienceverse/jw_plumber_bibr_api — Lisa DeBruine
  • f9da25f [dev] Merge pull request #332 from scienceverse/jw_bibr_read_passthrough — Lisa DeBruine
  • 0736a45 [dev] Merge pull request #331 from scienceverse/jw_grobid_text_fix — Lisa DeBruine
  • 9c2d675 [dev] Merge pull request #330 from scienceverse/jw_schema_sync_10_5 — Lisa DeBruine
  • 0a34c42 [data_check] process jasp files, better study categorization, better scale extraction. — Lakens
  • a4b8270 [data_check] additions — Lakens
  • 69afca1 [data_check] The OpenScales OSD export now exists end-to-end. Work spanned three files: — Lakens
  • c799dcf [data_check] improve cache, keep original data, better hardcoded folders — Lakens
  • 0c8303d [data_check] fix bugs, add archive report, gate large github repos, improve feedback — Lakens
  • 2615ab2 [data_check] updates osf download — Lakens
  • 58122ed [data_check] updates to functions, fix bugs — Lakens
  • ad4cb14 [data_check] improvements — Lakens
  • a73d52a [jw_llm_fixes] fix(llm): correct ollama-native gate (&& not %%); fail-fast param/model checks — Jakub Werner
  • d99956e [dev] feat(plumber): bibr-JSON REST API — validated uploads, HTML report, hardened runtime — Jakub Werner
  • 4daee8c [dev] feat(read): let bibr abstract and figure/table captions flow through — Jakub Werner
  • 00d98b8 [dev] fix(grobid): stop anchor normalization from corrupting paper text — Jakub Werner
  • 4d53313 [dev] fix(schema): sync bundled paper.json to bibr v10.5 — Jakub Werner
  • fdc5a1d [jw_fable_fixes] tests: fix test_that wrapper warnings, add expect_disjoint, env-aware .onLoad test — Jakub Werner

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronized it with `escicheckapp@7c4eb22`, which is related to "effectcheck v0.6.14", as stated in the commit message. This commit, numbered `#27`, brought the `main` branch in line with the specified version of `escicheckapp`. No other branches were worked on according to the provided commit messages. The specific change was to sync with a particular version, `7c4eb22`, of `escicheckapp`, which is associated with "effectcheck v0.6.14". Only the `main` branch is mentioned in the commit messages, with no other branches referenced.

1 commit(s) today:

  • e7b7351 [main] sync from escicheckapp@7c4eb22 (effectcheck v0.6.14) (#27) — Gilad Feldman
  • 7ec3c89 [main] sync from escicheckapp@ec9eb9f (effectcheck v0.6.13) (#26) — Gilad Feldman
  • e09dbc9 [main] sync from escicheckapp@edb3c7b (effectcheck v0.6.12) (#25) — Gilad Feldman
  • bedc262 [main] sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11) (#24) — Gilad Feldman
  • cc5cfd0 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1d4c2dd (effectcheck v0.6.9) (#23) — Gilad Feldman
  • 75ba643 [main] sync from escicheckapp@26f8430 (effectcheck v0.6.8) (#22) — Gilad Feldman
  • a154bdb [main] sync from escicheckapp@57a791d (effectcheck v0.6.7) (#21) — Gilad Feldman
  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
